skill.name: geo-fundamentals
skill.description: Generative Engine Optimization for AI search engines (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity).
skill.summary: Generative Engine Optimization skill for AI search engines with audit script
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged potential issues (external_commands, weak cryptographic algorithm) but manual review confirms these are false positives. The skill is documentation about GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) with a read-only Python script that analyzes local HTML/JSX/TSX files. No network requests, no command injection vectors, no cryptographic code present.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md lines 3, 66, 161. Manual review confirms NO cryptographic code exists. Lines contain only documentation text (YAML frontmatter, table content). This is a false positive from pattern matching.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic code exists in this skill. The scanner triggered on documentation text patterns, not actual cryptographic implementations.

medium_findings.title: External Commands (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at SKILL.md:157. Manual review shows this is documentation showing a Python command: 'python scripts/geo_checker.py <project_path>'. No actual command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 157 contains documentation for running a Python script, not backtick execution. The angle brackets in '<project_path>' triggered a false positive.

content.user_title: Optimize Content for AI Search Engines
content.value_statement: This skill helps content creators optimize their web pages for AI-powered search engines like ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity to increase citation rates in AI-generated responses.
actual_capabilities: Analyze web pages for AI citation readiness
Verify author attribution and publication dates
Detect FAQ sections and schema markup
Score pages on GEO best practices
limitations: Only analyzes HTML, JSX, and TSX files
Does not modify or write files

use_cases.title: Web Content Audit
use_cases.description: Run the geo_checker.py script on a project to analyze public-facing pages for AI optimization gaps.

use_cases.title: SEO Team Workflow
use_cases.description: Integrate GEO analysis into existing SEO workflows to ensure content is ready for AI engines.

use_cases.title: Pre-Publish Review
use_cases.description: Check new web pages for GEO elements before publishing to maximize AI citation potential.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze My Website
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the geo-fundamentals skill to audit my website at [PROJECT_PATH]. Check for structured data, author info, dates, and FAQ sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to check if your web pages are optimized for AI citation

prompt_templates.title: GEO Score Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the geo_checker.py script to get an average GEO score for my project. What elements are missing?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick score of your content optimization level

prompt_templates.title: Improve AI Citations
prompt_templates.prompt: Based on the GEO audit results, suggest specific changes to improve AI citation rates for my content.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want actionable recommendations to boost AI visibility

prompt_templates.title: Check Structured Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify if my web pages contain proper JSON-LD structured data for Article, FAQPage, and Organization schemas.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to verify technical SEO elements

output_examples.output:
GEO CHECKER - AI Citation Readiness Audit
Project: /path/to/my-website
Found 5 public pages to analyze
[OK] index.html: 75%
[OK] about.html: 80%
[!] contact.html: 55%
AVERAGE GEO SCORE: 70%
[OK] Good - Some improvements recommended

output_examples.input: Check my blog for GEO elements
output_examples.output:
Analysis shows your blog has:
- JSON-LD structured data: YES
- Author attribution: YES
- Publication dates: YES
- FAQ schema: NO
Recommendation: Add FAQ schema to increase citation probability in AI responses.

best_practices: Include structured data (JSON-LD) with Article and FAQPage schemas
Add clear author attribution with credentials
Use question-based headings and direct answer patterns
anti_patterns: Publishing content without dates or author info
Using thin content without original data or statistics
Skipping structured data markup for AI engines
Failing to add FAQ sections for direct answer extraction

faq.question: What is GEO?
faq.answer: GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) is the practice of optimizing content for AI-powered search engines like ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity to increase the likelihood of being cited in AI-generated responses.

faq.question: What does geo_checker.py analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes HTML, JSX, and TSX files for GEO elements including structured data, author info, publication dates, FAQ sections, and heading structure.

faq.question: Does this skill write or modify files?
faq.answer: No, the geo_checker.py script is read-only. It only reads and analyzes files without making any modifications.

faq.question: What is a good GEO score?
faq.answer: A score of 60% or above is considered passing. Scores above 80% indicate excellent optimization for AI citation.

faq.question: Which AI engines does this support?
faq.answer: The skill targets optimization for ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Gemini - the major AI-powered search engines.

faq.question: Does blocking AI crawlers affect GEO?
faq.answer: Yes, if you block AI crawlers (like GPTBot or Claude-Web) in robots.txt, your content will not be indexed by those AI engines and cannot be cited in responses.
